mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is clearly easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above the recent trend forces higher expected real rates and tighter policy expectations, which compresses equity multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month moving average and FEDFUNDS is below CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-breakout episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal loses sign consistency across two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and lower earnings expectations, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after a clear deterioration shock.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-shock days are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or if the sign flips after controlling for the past month of returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS signals resilient final demand and near-term revenue support, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when sales are accelerating from a weak base.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average by more than 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales acceleration episodes do not lead to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears in a 3-year rolling backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with contained CPIAUCSL reflects real output growth without strong inflation pressure, improving expected margins and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of positive INDPRO momentum combined with subdued CPIAUCSL do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if Rank IC remains under 0.03 on a rolling 2-year basis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST signals weakening residential demand and a broader slowdown in interest-sensitive spending, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if declining housing starts are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the housing signal has no incremental explanatory power versus a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a stable or falling UNRATE confirms broad labor-market expansion, which supports earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll gains with non-rising unemployment fail to outperform baseline 20-day returns, or if the joint signal is not stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% combined with elevated CPIAUCSL implies restrictive real rates and valuation compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate tightening episodes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect vanishes once CPIAUCSL is included.
